amends.amended_provision: labeler: single primary validator (Jack Maiorino) labels all 200 primary items

amended_to: Two named human labelers split the primary set: Jack Maiorino (110 items) and Joey Marcellino (110 items), sharing a deterministic 20-item overlap block labeled independently by both. Inter-rater agreement on the overlap is computed and reported (an upgrade over the single-owner design: it quantifies label reliability, mirroring the mechanism-audit's two-blind-pass precedent). Overlap disagreements are adjudicated by Jack Maiorino as primary validator, with the disagreement and adjudication recorded per item. The frozen label minimums (100 allow / 60 reject / 20 unresolved) and reserve-draw rule apply to the merged 200-item set unchanged.
rationale: All labels remain fully human; no model suggestions are shown to either labeler; the anti-anchoring suppression of synthetic/boundary metadata is preserved in both tools. The change reduces per-person burden while strictly adding information (inter-rater agreement).

owner_approval.approval_channel: direct chat request ('Can you split it for joey to label half and ill do the other half?') and confirmation of the overlap design in the same exchange

oracle_review: Codex unavailable until 2026-07-24 (usage limit); post-hoc review REQUIRED and scheduled before the merged labels are consumed by checker-candidate selection; consumption is blocked until that review passes
second_labeler_note: Joey Marcellino also fills the design's previously-null second-human slot; the accepted_query_auditor assignment for live-run audits remains open
note: Append-only amendment; the original design file is never rewritten.
